Where is the origin of chocolate?

HuffPost reported that cacao trees, which produce cocoa beans, originated in the tropical regions of Central and South America, specifically in the Amazon Basin and parts of Mesoamerica. Over time, chocolate spread to Europe and eventually became popular around the world.
